title: Lab 41: IPv6 Networking
description: Configure persistent IPv6 networking and hostname resolution on the client system

tasks:
On client, configure the active client connection with the following persistent IPv6 settings:

IPv6 Address: fd00:122:41::25/64
IPv6 Gateway: fd00:122:41::1
DNS: fd00:122:41::53

On client, set the following persistent identity and resolution settings:

Hostname: client.ipv6lab.local
Static host entry: server.ipv6lab.local
Address: fd00:122:41::3
hints: Use nmcli so the change survives a reconnect.
checks: nmcli connection show "System eth1" >/dev/null && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.addresses connection show "System eth1" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::25/64' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.gateway connection show "System eth1" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.dns connection show "System eth1" | tr -d '\\')" = 'fd00:122:41::53' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv6.method connection show "System eth1")" = 'manual'
hostnamectl --static | grep -qx 'client.ipv6lab.local' && getent hosts server.ipv6lab.local | grep -Fq 'fd00:122:41::3'
solution_commands: nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv6.method manual ipv6.addresses fd00:122:41::25/64 ipv6.gateway fd00:122:41::1 ipv6.dns fd00:122:41::53 connection.autoconnect yes
